component.name: Cheat Engine for Linux
component.summary: Memory scanner, debugger, and injector for modding games and apps

description.p: A Linux-native reimplementation of Cheat Engine in C++ and Qt6: memory scanning, pointer scanning, an auto-assembler, an interactive multi-thread debugger, structure dissection, cheat tables, and Lua scripting.
Focused on single-player game modding and reverse engineering. It does not target multiplayer anti-cheat.

Full CLI / headless parity: every GUI tool is now scriptable from the terminal through the Lua API and the cescan headless runner, so features are testable without the GUI. New bindings for pointer scan, structure dissect, managed-runtime detection, find-what-writes/accesses, break-and-trace, branch mapper, register/stack read-write, find-statics, cheat-table save/load, trainer generation, ceserver connect, and address-list grouping. Also fixes a trainer code-generation bug (invalid float literal for whole-number values).

Complete interactive debugger (software + hardware breakpoints, register editing, XMM, thread switcher, memory pane, scriptable Lua debugger); cecore can act as a ceserver for remote memory access; untrusted cheat-table security hardening.
AOB-injection fix, 32-bit/WoW64/Wine support, cheat-table inline editing.
